title: Citrusy Chewy Taro Stir-fry
description: A lazy Sunday Filipino dish featuring a citrusy twist with Chinese sesame sauce and a tadka for extra flavor. This nut-free-friendly dish is perfect as a side or light meal.

ingredients.name: orange zest
ingredients.notes: freshly squeezed or from a few oranges

ingredients.name: ginger slices
ingredients.notes: optional for added depth of flavor

instructions: Heat a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sesame oil and ground fennel seeds (if using), stir-fry for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add garlic cloves, ginger slices if using, orange zest, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and cumin powder to the wok. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
Gently add the cubed taro into the wok. Toss everything together for about 2 minutes or until the taro is tender but still has a chewy texture.
Turn off heat if tadka was used, stir in the sesame oil and mix thoroughly to coat the taro evenly.
Serve hot as a side dish or lightly drizzle with extra sesame oil for garnish.
